page: Google Cloud Platform a3-edgegpu-8g-nolssd is an accelerator-optimized server configured with eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs from the Hopper architecture, providing a total of 637 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor offering 208 vCPUs across 104 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It features 1872.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 9.0 GB of RAM per core. A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local SSD storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage solutions.

Benchmark data highlights the massive parallel processing capabilities of this configuration. The server achieves top-tier multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 13662.82 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 430.93 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Cryptographic and database operations also rank in the top tier, with Redis SET operations exceeding 177 million operations per second. Ray tracing benchmarks further confirm the high-performance profile of the underlying hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server represents a high-density hardware profile designed for specialized, resource-intensive tasks where GPU throughput is the primary driver of value. The lack of local storage reduces local disk maintenance overhead but shifts storage architecture design to network-based options. This server is optimized for large-scale machine learning training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing, and complex scientific simulations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a3-edgegpu-8g-nolssd is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel computing workloads. It features eight NVIDIA H100 GPUs with 637 GB of total VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor that delivers 208 vCPUs and 1872.0 GB of system memory. This configuration does not include local SSD storage, necessitating network-attached storage alternatives. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Given its high-density hardware profile, it offers qualitative cost efficiency for specialized, compute-heavy tasks where GPU acceleration is critical. It is highly suited for deep learning, large-scale AI training, and high-performance computing simulations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-edgegpu-8g-nolssd is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 8 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 208 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1872 GB RAM, designed without local SSD storage.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a3-edgegpu-8g-nolssd features 8 NVIDIA H100 GPUs, 208 vCPUs, and 1872 GB RAM, optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized server featuring eight high-performance GPUs and massive system memory for intensive parallel computing and AI workloads.
bullet_points: Eight NVIDIA H100 Hopper GPUs with 637 GB total VRAM
208 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors
1872.0 GB of system memory with high-bandwidth throughput
No local SSD storage, requiring network-attached storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks
Designed for large-scale AI training and high-performance computing
